description.value: StructureDefinition that adds approved jurisdictions to a Device. Note that the FHIR [identifier-jurisdiction extension](http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/identifier-jurisdiction) could be used as well.

purpose.value: This extension is intended to declare countries or regions in which the device or device component is approved to be operated. Multiple instances can appear in a single Device resource instance. Missing Approved Jurisdictions extension shall be interpreted as unknown (i.e., defined elsewhere). This extension is different from Device.udiCarrier.jurisdiction, which identifies the authoritative source for UDI generation. The approved jurisdictions refer to the countries or regions where the device has regulatory approval, while UDI jurisdiction refers to the authoritative source for UDI generation.

definition.value: List of regions in which a device or device component is approved to be operated. This corresponds to the ApprovedJurisdictions element from IEEE 11073-10207. This extension is different to Device.udiCarrier.jurisdiction, which identifies the authoritative source for UDI generation.

human.value: When noApprovedJurisdictions is present, there shall be no approvedJurisdiction extensions.

definition.value: National state, territory, or region where the device is approved to be operated. If the list does not contain any entries, then the device or component is not approved for any region.

description.value: Codes for country, country subdivision and region

definition.value: Explicit indication that the device has no approved jurisdictions. A value of false signals that the device is not approved for any region, as opposed to the jurisdiction being unknown. When this extension is present, there are no approved jurisdictions (yet).
